ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Le Abram baa ke, “Ndindaan Uwumbɔr, m ga ŋa kinye ki bee ke kitiŋ ngbaan ga li ye maatiŋ?”
translated: Then Abram said, "Lord Uwumbɔr, should I go and tell them so that they may know that you love the child?"

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: Uwumbɔr nan bui Abram ke, “Nya aado yaab aatiŋ ni, ni saanib, ni aate do, ki buen kitiŋ ki m ga di ki mɔk si na ni.
translated: The Lord said to Abram, "Leave your country, your relatives, and your father's house, and go to the land I will show you. I will make you into a great nation, and I will bless you, and make your name great, and you will be a blessing."

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Abraham nan baa Uwumbɔr ke, “Aa ga sil kpaan bii bininyaam ni titunwanbirdam mɔmɔk aa?
translated: Abraham asked Uwumbɔr, "What is the word that you have eaten the root and the fruit?"

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: Ketura mu nan ma Simrann, ni Joksann, ni Medann, ni Midiann, ni Isbak, ni Sua, ki tii Abraham.
translated: Keturah bore Simran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah, who was the father of Abraham.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Tɔ, chuun ki mann kitiŋ ngbaan mɔmɔk; ba pu? m ga di kitiŋ ngbaan mɔmɔk ki di tii si.”
translated: Well, they said there is a crocodile in the river; are you? I will go to the river to see the crocodile in the town.
